The U.S. negotiation team, headed by Steve Witkoff, met on Tuesday morning Geneva time with Badr Al-Busaidi, Oman's Foreign Minister, and the Director General of the IAEA - reports

data.title: Apple is launching video podcast feature on Apple Podcasts app, empowering creators with control over content. Picture-in-picture support and HLS format included for improved user experience. No content distribution fee by Apple.
data.description: Tech giant Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) is set to launch a fresh video podcast experience on its Apple Podcasts app in the upcoming spring season. Senior Vice President of Services, Eddy Cue, shared that this new feature aims to give creators more autonomy over their content and revenue models, as well as enhance the overall user experience by providing seamless transitions between audio and video podcasts. The update will encompass picture-in-picture functionality and allow users to download video episodes for offline enjoyment. Even though Apple Podcasts has supported video content through RSS since 2005, this update will introduce support for HLS (HTTP Live Streaming), Apple's proprietary streaming protocol, offering adaptive playback and better in-app video controls. The HLS format will enable dynamic video ad insertion, facilitating creators to include video ads through partnering hosts and ad networks. While Apple will not charge for content distribution, it will levy an impression-based fee.

data.title: Vow ASA (OSE: VOW) to share Q4 2025 financial results on Feb 25, 2026 via newsweb.oslobors.no and vowasa.com.
data.description: VOW ASA, trading under the ticker symbol VOW on the Oslo Stock Exchange, is scheduled to announce its fourth quarter financial results for 2025 on Wednesday, February 25, 2026. The report will be made available on both https://newsweb.oslobors.no/ and the company's official website at www.vowasa.com.

data.title: Nexo reenters US market in 2026 with compliant framework and partnership with Bakkt Inc. for digital asset services.
data.description: Cryptocurrency lending platform Nexo has announced its return to the U.S. market in 2026, following a three-year hiatus after settling an SEC lawsuit. The company stated it will reenter with a U.S.-compliant framework for its investment and credit product offerings through a partnership with regulated entities. The digital asset trading infrastructure will be provided by Bakkt Inc. (NYSE: BKKT), a U.S.-based publicly listed firm. Nexo will introduce a range of digital asset services in the U.S., including flexible and fixed-term yield programs, a cryptocurrency trading exchange, crypto-backed credit lines, and a loyalty program.

data.title: Nuclera and leadXpro collaborate to accelerate drug design for membrane proteins using eProtein Discovery™ and AI/ML.
data.description: Nuclera, a biotech company from Cambridge, England, and leadXpro, a specialist in structure-based drug discovery from Boston, announced a partnership in Villigen, Switzerland. This collaboration combines Nuclera's technology for quick access to high-quality proteins with leadXpro's expertise in studying membrane proteins. The partnership aims to advance structural studies and therapeutic development by integrating eProtein Discovery’s rapid multiplex membrane protein screening with leadXpro’s AI/ML-driven construct design. This collaboration is expected to enhance research efficiency and possibly lead to groundbreaking discoveries in the field.

data.title: METCO signs localization agreements at World Defense Show in Riyadh, bolstering defense capabilities; Feb. 17, 2026.
data.description: In Riyadh, Saudi Arabia on February 17, 2026, METCO, a Saudi-based group offering integrated solutions and services, announced the signing of strategic localization agreements at the World Defense Show. These agreements highlight METCO's commitment to aiding in the localization of defense and security capabilities.

data.title: C&A partners with Nedap to improve omnichannel operations in Europe, enhancing Click & Collect efficiency.
data.description: Improved visibility of individual items in inventory is enhancing the efficiency of Click & Collect (BOPIS) services throughout Europe. This increased visibility of item-level inventory is transforming the way customers shop, with a significant increase in efficiency and convenience. The streamlined process allows customers to easily select and collect items they have ordered online, saving time and reducing wait times. This improvement in inventory visibility has resulted in a notable decrease in order processing times by up to 30%, significantly benefiting both customers and retailers.

data.title: Vaimo names Woody Anderson Sr. Enterprise Account Exec in North America. Focus: Growing Vaimo's enterprise footprint.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, Vaimo, a prominent company specializing in digital commerce and experience solutions, revealed the selection of Woody Anderson for the position of Senior Enterprise Account Executive for North America. Woody's main objective in this role will be to expand Vaimo's enterprise presence in the region.

data.title: SalMar issues NOK 750 million senior unsecured green bond with 10-year tenor, 5.625% coupon rate. Rated BBB+/Negative Outlook.
data.description: SalMar ASA, a company with a BBB+ credit rating and Negative Outlook from Nordic Credit Rating, has recently issued a new senior unsecured green bond worth NOK 750 million. The bond has a 10-year term and carries a coupon rate of 5.625%, which is equivalent to a floating rate of 3mN+150 basis points.

data.title: UGREEN is launching new AI-powered NAS models, revolutionizing private cloud storage. Redefining data management.
data.description: UGREEN revealed today that they will soon release their new flagship AI NAS models in London on February 17, 2026. These models will revolutionize private cloud storage by incorporating artificial intelligence, transforming data management. Instead of functioning as passive storage solutions, UGREEN's latest offerings will actively enhance user experience.
